# **1\. Introduction** {#1.-introduction}

## **1.1. Overview** {#1.1.-overview}

Singapore is in the middle of an unprecedented retail trading boom. As of 2025, Moomoo Singapore alone has surpassed 1.5 million users in a country of roughly 5.9 million people, meaning approximately one in four Singaporeans now holds an account on a single retail brokerage platform.¹ Tiger Brokers’ parent UP Fintech reported full-year 2025 revenue of US$612.1 million, up 56.3% year-on-year, with Singapore and Hong Kong together accounting for roughly 80% of new funded accounts.²

However, the democratization of access has not been matched by a democratization of analytical capability. Free platforms such as Yahoo Finance and TradingView provide raw data and charting but offer no predictive insight. Institutional-grade platforms such as Bloomberg Terminal deliver sophisticated analytics but cost US$31,980 per single-seat user per year, placing them out of reach of retail investors.³ A third category of low-cost AI-driven tools, exemplified by services such as Danelfin, attempts to fill the middle ground but presents predictions as opaque scores with little explanation of methodology or accuracy.

This three-way gap presents a clear opportunity to apply modern, open-source machine learning techniques to deliver predictive analytics that are affordable, capable, and transparent. StockLens is conceived to occupy this underserved space: a web-based platform providing retail investors with machine-learning-driven price predictions and trading signals while making the underlying model performance, confidence, and historical accuracy fully visible. Rather than positioning itself as a replacement for professional advice, StockLens is designed as a transparent alternative to black-box prediction tools, empowering users to make better-informed decisions while remaining within the educational scope required by Singapore’s Monetary Authority Financial Advisers Act.

![][image3]

*Figure 1.1: Market positioning of StockLens against existing retail investing tools.*

## **1.2. Project Scope** {#1.2.-project-scope}

StockLens is a full-stack web application that ingests historical end-of-day equity data, trains and serves machine learning models, and presents predictions to retail investors through a responsive browser interface. Coverage at launch is limited to the major United States exchanges (NYSE and NASDAQ), reflecting the fact that Singapore retail investors overwhelmingly trade US equities through platforms such as Moomoo and Tiger Brokers. Singapore Exchange (SGX) coverage is treated as a stretch objective.

For each supported stock, the system produces a predicted next-day closing price together with a directional buy, sell, or hold signal. The predicted price provides an intuitive headline figure for users, while the directional signal is the metric on which the models are honestly evaluated. Every prediction is accompanied by a model performance dashboard reporting Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Directional Accuracy, and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) for cross-stock comparison.

| Core Scope | Stretch Objectives | Out of Scope |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Data pipeline (yfinance \+ Supabase) | SGX market coverage | Real-time / intraday streaming data |
| XGBoost \+ LSTM models | Ensemble model (XGBoost+LSTM+Sentiment) | Automated order execution / live trading |
| Sentiment analysis (VADER / FinBERT) | SHAP explainability dashboard | Options, futures, FX, crypto |
| React+Vite frontend, Flask API | PWA mobile packaging | Personalised financial advice |
| Role-based access control (4 tiers) |   | Native iOS / Android apps |
| MAS regulatory disclaimers |   | Multi-language support |
| Performance dashboard (RMSE, MAE, DA, MAPE) |   | Automated model retraining |

*Table 1.1: Project scope summary across core deliverables, stretch objectives, and exclusions.*

Within the core scope, the platform ships role-based authentication via Supabase supporting four user types (Guest, Free Registered, Premium, Administrator). No live payment processing is implemented during the FYP timeline; the tier structure exists as functional access control to demonstrate the commercial model described in Section 1.4. Every prediction screen carries a notice that the output is for educational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice, in compliance with the Monetary Authority of Singapore Financial Advisers Act.

## **1.3. Project Description** {#1.3.-project-description}

![][image4]

*Figure 1.2: StockLens system architecture and data flow.*

StockLens is delivered as a single-page web application built on a React and Vite frontend, a Python Flask backend exposing a RESTful API, and a Supabase (PostgreSQL) database providing relational storage and built-in authentication. Historical price data is sourced through the yfinance library, and machine learning models are developed using scikit-learn for the XGBoost baseline and Keras for the LSTM network. The platform is deployed to free-tier cloud infrastructure suitable for an academic project, with specific tooling detailed in Section 6\.

A typical interaction follows a Free Registered user, the platform’s primary segment. After signing in, the user searches for a stock by ticker or company name and is taken to a prediction screen showing the next-day predicted closing price, a buy, sell, or hold signal, and a confidence indicator. Below the headline figure sits an interactive chart comparing historical predicted prices against actual outcomes, allowing the user to visually verify how the model has performed on this stock over time. A model performance panel reports RMSE, MAE, Directional Accuracy, and MAPE, so the user can judge for themselves whether the prediction deserves their attention. If they want to track the stock, they add it to a watchlist of up to ten tickers and receive its updated prediction each time they return.

What ties these features into a coherent product is a single editorial principle: every screen that shows a prediction also shows how often that model has been right. This same principle removes the need for users to juggle separate tools for charting, predictions, sentiment, and watchlists, replacing a fragmented workflow with a single transparent dashboard.

## **1.4. Business Model** {#1.4.-business-model}

StockLens adopts the Business Model Canvas framework to articulate its commercial structure. The canvas below summarises the nine blocks; the subsections that follow expand on the user-facing elements in line with the structure of comparable Final Year Project documents.

| KEY PARTNERS yfinance / Yahoo Finance, Vercel, Render, Supabase, UOW supervisor, OSS ML community | KEY ACTIVITIES ML model training & evaluation, daily data pipeline, web platform maintenance, transparent performance reporting | VALUE PROPOSITIONS Free ML predictions, transparent model metrics, sentiment overlay, unified retail-investor workflow |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **KEY RESOURCES** 5-person dev team, yfinance data, free-tier cloud, OSS libraries, academic supervision | **CUSTOMER RELATIONSHIPS** Self-service web app, transparent metrics build trust, community engagement on Reddit / LinkedIn | **CHANNELS** Direct web access, organic discovery in finance / ML communities, FYP showcase, word-of-mouth |
| **COST STRUCTURE** FYP: \~$0 cash outlay (free tiers \+ student time). Commercial: paid hosting, data feeds, ML compute, ops & compliance. |  | **CUSTOMER SEGMENTS** Retail investors 25–45 (primary), finance/CS students (secondary), active hobbyist traders (tertiary) |
| **REVENUE STREAMS** Freemium SaaS: Guest (free, 3/day), Free Registered (free, watchlist), Premium (paid, full feature set) |  |  |

*Table 1.2: StockLens Business Model Canvas summary.*

**1.4.1 Value Propositions**  
Building on the three-way market gap identified in Section 1.1, StockLens delivers four concrete value points: free machine learning predictions for retail investors who cannot justify institutional pricing; transparent model performance reported on every prediction screen, in contrast to black-box scoring tools; a sentiment overlay drawn from public news and social media, which free retail tools currently lack; and a unified workflow that replaces the typical retail investor’s fragmented mix of charting, news, and watchlist apps.

**1.4.2 Individual Users (Consumers)**  
StockLens primarily targets individual retail investors aged 25 to 45, who form the largest and fastest-growing segment of the Singapore retail trading market as evidenced by the platform user numbers cited in Section 1.1. These users are typically employed professionals using mobile brokerages such as Tiger Brokers and Moomoo, who want clearer, data-driven signals to support their own investment decisions but cannot justify institutional-grade tooling. A secondary individual segment comprises finance and Computer Science students who use the platform as an educational lens onto applied quantitative finance, and a tertiary segment of active hobbyist traders who currently juggle multiple free tools and would adopt a unified, sentiment-aware dashboard.

**1.4.3 Business Users**  
While the FYP delivery is consumer-facing, the platform is structured to support a future business-to-business path. Potential business users include independent financial educators and content creators who could use the transparent performance dashboard as teaching material, small wealth advisory firms that could embed StockLens predictions into their client reporting, and fintech partners who could license the prediction API. These segments are not pursued during the FYP timeline but inform the architecture decisions, in particular the decision to expose predictions through a clean RESTful API rather than coupling them tightly to the frontend.

**1.4.4 Channels**

1. Direct web access through the deployed application URL, optimised for desktop and mobile browsers.  
2. Organic discovery in finance and machine learning communities on Reddit (such as r/SingaporeInvestments and r/algotrading), Discord servers, and LinkedIn.  
3. Academic showcases through the University of Wollongong Final Year Project demonstration day and the project website.  
4. Word-of-mouth referrals from early adopters in the Singapore retail investing community.  
   

**1.4.5 Revenue Stream**  
StockLens adopts a freemium SaaS revenue model with three user tiers. As an academic project, no real payments are processed during the FYP timeline; the tiered structure is implemented as functional access control to demonstrate a commercially viable path.

***Freemium Access (Guest and Free Registered)***

Guest users may access the platform without registering and are limited to three predictions per day, providing a low-friction acquisition channel. Free Registered users create an account in exchange for unlimited predictions and a watchlist of up to ten tickers, supporting both engagement and email capture for future marketing.

***Premium Access***

Premium users unlock the portfolio tracker, the sentiment news feed, price alerts, and the stock comparison feature, offered as a monthly or annual subscription priced competitively against existing retail analytics tools.

| Feature | Guest | Free Registered | Premium |
| :---- | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **Account required** | No | Yes | Yes |
| **Predictions per day** | 3 | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| **Watchlist** | — | Up to 10 | Unlimited |
| **Performance dashboard** | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| **Sentiment news feed** | — | — | Yes |
| **Portfolio tracker** | — | — | Yes |
| **Price alerts** | — | — | Yes |
| **Stock comparison** | — | — | Yes |
| **Price** | Free | Free | Paid |

*Table 1.3: Feature comparison across the three StockLens user tiers.*

**1.4.6 Cost Structure**

***FYP costs***

Development is covered by student time. Hosting, compute, and database run within Vercel, Render, and Supabase free tiers. Data is free through yfinance. Net cash outlay for the project is effectively zero, with only minor expenses for domain registration and presentation tooling. A 10% contingency buffer is reserved for unforeseen API rate-limit upgrades or paid sentiment data sources should free alternatives prove insufficient.

***Commercial costs***

A launched version would incur paid cloud hosting that scales with user volume, paid market data subscriptions for higher-frequency or wider-coverage feeds, ongoing ML training compute, and standard operational costs for support, marketing, and compliance. These costs are noted here for completeness; full financial modelling is out of scope for this PRD.

**1.4.7 Customer Segments**  
Synthesizing the user discussion above, StockLens addresses three customer segments in priority order. The primary segment is retail investors aged 25 to 45 already active on mobile brokerages, validated by the Singapore market data in Section 1.1. The secondary segment is finance and Computer Science students seeking an educational tool that demonstrates applied quantitative methods. The tertiary segment is active hobbyist traders frustrated with the fragmentation of existing free tools. Each segment is reachable through the channels listed above and benefits from the same core value propositions, with the transparent performance dashboard serving as the unifying feature across all three.

# **2\. Research Finding Analysis** {#2.-research-finding-analysis}

## **2.1. Applications in the market** {#2.1.-applications-in-the-market}

![][image5]

## **2.2. Research Summary**  {#2.2.-research-summary}

**2.2.1 Overview of Stock Market Prediction** 

Stock market prediction is a complex and widely studied problem in both finance and computer science due to the dynamic and stochastic nature of financial markets. Stock prices are influenced by a wide range of factors including historical trends, macroeconomic indicators, investor sentiment, and unexpected global events. As a result, accurately predicting stock prices is inherently difficult, and most research focuses on identifying probabilistic trends rather than exact values.

With the rapid growth of data availability and computational power, modern approaches increasingly rely on machine learning and web-based systems to process large datasets and deliver insights to users in real time. Web services play a critical role in this ecosystem by enabling scalable, accessible, and platform-independent delivery of predictive analytics to end users.

**2.2.2 Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models**

To address the limitations of traditional models, researchers have adopted machine learning techniques.

One of the most widely used models is Long Short-Term Memory, a type of recurrent neural network designed to capture sequential dependencies in time-series data. LSTM models are particularly effective in stock prediction because they can learn long-term patterns and handle non-linear relationships.

Another powerful approach is XGBoost, which is widely used for structured data prediction tasks. XGBoost has been shown to achieve high accuracy in predicting stock price direction and reducing prediction errors such as RMSE and MAE.

Comparative studies suggest that no single model consistently outperforms others across all scenarios. Instead:

* LSTM captures temporal and non-linear patterns   
* XGBoost performs well in classification and feature-based prediction 

As a result, many modern systems adopt hybrid or ensemble approaches that combine multiple models to improve performance.

**2.2.3 Role of Sentiment Analysis**

Recent research highlights the importance of incorporating external information such as news and social media sentiment into stock prediction systems.

Sentiment Analysis is used to extract opinions and emotions from textual data. These sentiments can influence investor behaviour and short-term market movements.

Advanced models such as FinBERT are specifically designed for financial text analysis. Studies show that combining sentiment analysis with machine learning models like LSTM can significantly improve prediction accuracy, especially in short-term forecasting.

**2.2.4 Web Services in Stock Market Prediction Systems**

Web services play a crucial role in modern stock prediction platforms by enabling real-time data processing, model deployment, and user interaction through web-based interfaces.

A typical architecture involves:

* Data ingestion from APIs (e.g., market data providers)   
* Backend processing using machine learning models   
* RESTful APIs for communication   
* Frontend dashboards for visualization 

Technologies such as Flask and React are commonly used to build scalable and responsive systems.

Research shows that web-based platforms improve accessibility by allowing users to interact with predictive models without needing technical expertise, making them suitable for retail investors.

**2.2.5 Evaluation Metrics**

To evaluate prediction performance, several metrics are commonly used:

* **Mean Absolute Error (MAE)** – measures average error magnitude   
* **Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE)** – penalises larger errors   
* **Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)** – expresses error in percentage form   
* **Directional Accuracy (DA)** – measures correctness of predicted price movement 

Among these, directional accuracy is particularly important in financial applications, as correctly predicting whether a stock price will increase or decrease is often more valuable than predicting the exact price.

**2.2.6 Limitations in Existing Research**

Despite advancements, several limitations remain:

* Stock markets are inherently unpredictable due to external factors   
* Models may suffer from overfitting and poor generalisation   
* Many systems operate as “black boxes” with limited transparency   
* Existing tools often lack user-friendly interfaces for retail investors 

Furthermore, most research focuses on improving model accuracy rather than delivering practical, interpretable solutions through accessible platforms.

**2.2.7 Research Gap and Project Contribution**

Based on the literature, there is a clear gap between advanced predictive models and their practical usability for retail investors.

This project addresses this gap by developing a web-based system that integrates:

* Multiple machine learning models (LSTM, XGBoost)   
* Sentiment analysis for enhanced prediction   
* Transparent performance metrics (RMSE, MAE, MAPE, DA)   
* A user-friendly dashboard for visualization and interaction 

Unlike existing solutions, the system emphasizes **transparency and interpretability**, allowing users to evaluate model performance rather than relying on opaque predictions. This bridges the gap between academic research and real-world financial decision support tools.

## **2.3. AI-Powered Stock Market Prediction** {#2.3.-ai-powered-stock-market-prediction}

The adoption of artificial intelligence in the financial markets has produced a growing landscape of platforms that apply machine learning to stock market predictions. This section reviews the main commercially available platforms, compares their models and verified performance metrics, explains how Monte Carlo simulation complements them through probabilistic forecasting, and concludes by assessing the alternative data and ensemble strategies behind the most effective systems.

**2.3.1 Commercially Available AI Prediction Platforms**  
The market for AI-powered stock prediction tools has expanded significantly over the recent years. According to Precedence Research, the global AI trading platform market was valued at approximately 11.26 billion USD in 2024 and is projected to reach 69.95 billion by 2034 (Biz4Group, 2026). The platforms reviewed below represent the most widely adopted tools currently available.

**Danelfin** is a Finnish AI stock analytics platform that processes over 10,000 features per stock daily including 600 technical indicators, 150 fundamental metrics and 150 sentiment signals, distilling them into a single AI score from 1-10. The score reflects the probability that a stock will outperform the market over the next three months. To generate this score, Danelfin uses hundreds of decision trees that combines its “alpha signals” to estimate the probability that a stock or ETF will outperform the market. According to published performance data, stocks rated 10/10 have outperformed the market by an average of 21.05% after three months since 2017, while stocks rated 1/10 have underperformed by 33.28%. The platform’s best stocks strategy generated a return of \+376% from January 2017 to June 2025, compared to \+166% for the S\&P 500 (Danelfin.com, 2025; GitHub/uvk870, 2025). A key differentiator is their use of their own AI, Explainable AI: each score is decomposed into visible technical, fundamental and sentiment sub-scores, which eliminates the opacity of conventional black-box models. The platform also integrates directly with online brokerage and trading platforms like Interactive Brokers, Tradestation and Webull (Danelfin.com, 2025; GitHub/uvk870, 2025).

![][image6]

*Figure 1.3: Danelfin’s Explainable AI breakdown.*

**Numerai** is a crowd-sourced AI hedge fund that invites data scientists worldwide to submit ML model predictions on financial data. Individual submissions are aggregated into a stake-weighted meta model that drives all trading decisions. The approach embodies ensemble learning at a scale: diverse, independently trained models from thousands of contributors are combined, reducing individual model bias and variance. Numerai returned 20% to investors during the 2022 market downturn, a year that saw significant lossess across most conventional strategies. This demonstrates the resilience of the ensemble approach. (Wikipedia, 2025; Insider Monkey, 2025). Participants typically use LightGBM, XGBoost, and neural networks, with the best-performing contributors earning rewards in the platform's NMR cryptocurrency.

**Trade Ideas (Holly AI)** is an AI-powered real-time stock scanner built primarily for day traders. Its core engine, Holly, runs overnight simulations on millions of stock configurations, then presents the highest-probability trade setups each morning before the market opens. Holly scans millions of data points, news feeds, and price movements to identify high-probability trading opportunities, and users can view curated trade ideas, set alerts, and test strategies in a simulated environment before committing capital (Biz4Group, 2026). It is among the most widely adopted tools among active retail day traders in the US market.

**Tickeron** combines AI pattern recognition with a Trend Prediction Engine (TPE) that claims approximately 70% accuracy on short-term directional calls across a coverage universe of 7,000 stocks, 10,000 OTC stocks, 3,000 ETFs, and 14,000 mutual funds. Its AI Robot Marketplace allows users to browse and follow independently built trading bots, each with a publicly viewable performance track record — some of which have posted annualised returns above 100%, though as with all backtested results, independent verification is important (WallStreetZen, 2026).

**VantagePoint AI** uses neural networks and intermarket analysis to forecast stock direction several days ahead by analysing correlations between a target stock and related markets — including indices, sectors, currencies, and commodities. The platform claims up to 86% directional prediction accuracy and has been in operation since 1991, providing a longer live track record than many newer competitors (Cadchain, 2025). It is particularly suited to traders of commodities, ETFs, and futures.

**Prospero.ai** combines institutional options flow data, social media sentiment, and fundamental signals into a unified ML scoring system. The platform publicly discloses a four-year average S\&P 500 beat of 67%, with individual picks in 2025 winning versus the S\&P 500 at a 60% rate and beating the index by 63% annualised as of November 2025 (WallStreetZen, 2026). Unlike most platforms, Prospero provides its core signals free via mobile app and newsletter, making institutional-grade data accessible to retail investors.

**I Know Firs**t applies a self-learning algorithm inspired by chaos theory and genetic algorithms to produce daily forecasts for over 13,500 financial instruments. Published evaluation reports cite accuracy rates of up to 88–97% on S\&P 500 and Nasdaq directional calls over specific evaluation periods. The platform's live forecasts for the Japanese stock market have continuously outperformed the Nikkei 225 benchmark, and its MSCI Stock Universe model has outperformed the MSCI-ACWI Index and ACWI ETF benchmarks (IKnowFirst.com, 2025).

**2.3.2 Structured Comparisons of Market Platforms**  
The table below provides a structured comparison of the major commercially available AI stock prediction platforms.

| Platform | Core ML Model | Reported Performance | Best For | Pricing |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Danelfin | Deep learning; 10,000+ features daily | \+376% (2017–Jun 2025\) vs S\&P 500 \+166%; AI Score 10/10 stocks \+21% alpha (3-month) | Swing traders; short-to-mid-term picks | Free / $29–$79/month |
| Kavout | Deep neural networks; Kai Score (1–9) | Conservative port. \+11.6% p.a.; Aggressive \+20.8% p.a. (since 2020\) | Portfolio builders; institutional-grade research | Free / $16–$39/month |
| Numerai | Crowdsourced ML ensemble (Meta Model) | 20% return in 2022 market downturn; consistently beats benchmark | Quant researchers; hedge fund strategies | Free to submit; stake NMR |
| Trade Ideas (Holly AI) | ML pattern scanning; rule-based AI | Scans millions of data points daily; widely used by day traders | Day traders; real-time alerts | $999–$1,999/yr |
| Tickeron | Pattern recognition \+ trend prediction engine | \~70% TPE accuracy (short-term); some robots \>100% annualized | Active traders; multi-asset coverage | $60/yr–$250/month |
| VantagePoint AI | Neural networks; intermarket analysis | Up to 86% directional accuracy claimed | Trend forecasting; commodities & ETFs | Custom pricing |
| Prospero.ai | Options flow \+ sentiment \+ fundamental ML | 67% 4-yr avg S\&P beat; 60% win rate (2025 picks) | Retail investors; signal-based strategies | Free / newsletter tiers |
| I Know First | Self-learning algorithm; chaos theory \+ ML | Up to 88–97% accuracy (S\&P 500 & Nasdaq reports) | Institutional; customized forecasts | Enterprise pricing |

# 

# **3\. Project Motivation** {#3.-project-motivation}

The motivation for this project comes from the growing importance of data-driven decision-making in the financial market, where investors are expected to respond quickly to large volumes of dynamic and continuously changing information. Stock market prices are influenced by many factors, including historical trends, market sentiment, and real-time events such as tweets or posts made by influential political figures. As a result, manual analysis is often impractical, time-consuming, and insufficient for timely decision making. At the same time, advances in web technologies and machine learning have created opportunities to build intelligent systems that can process market data more efficiently and present useful insights through accessible online platforms. This project is therefore motivated by the need to bridge the gap between complex stock market analysis and practical user support by developing a webservice that integrates real-time data access, predictive modelling, and recommendation functions within a single system. It also provides an opportunity to apply knowledge of software development, data processing, and machine learning to solve a realistic problem with strong relevance to modern financial technology.

# **4\. Functional Requirements** {#4.-functional-requirements}

## **4.1 Functional Hierarchy** {#4.1-functional-hierarchy}

![][image7]

![][image8]

## **4.2 Preliminary Use Case Diagram** {#4.2-preliminary-use-case-diagram}

**Unregistered User**

![][image9]

**User Admin**

![][image10]

**Backend Admin**

![][image11]

**Base User**

![][image12]

**Premium User**

![][image13]

## **4.3 Preliminary Use Case Description** {#4.3-preliminary-use-case-description}

This section provides detailed descriptions for each use case identified in Section 4.2. Each description follows a standard template containing the use case name, identifier, stakeholders and goals, a brief description, actors, trigger, pre-conditions, main flow, sub-flow, and alternative or exceptional flow. Use cases are grouped by actor role. The descriptions below cover the Unregistered User role; descriptions for the remaining roles are provided by other team members.

**4.3.1 Unregistered User Use Case Description**

| Name: Register User Account | ID: \#1 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Unregistered User – To create a personal Base User account that unlocks unlimited stock predictions, a favourites list of up to ten tickers, and personalized features on the StockLens platform. |  |
| **Description:** An unregistered user fills in a registration form to create a new Base User account on the StockLens platform. Upon successful registration, the user is automatically signed in and granted Base User privileges. Upgrades to Premium are handled separately through the “Subscribe to Premium” use case and are not part of the registration process. |  |
| **Actors:** Unregistered User / Guest |  |
| **Trigger:** When the user clicks the “Register” or “Sign Up” button on the landing page. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The user is not already registered with the provided email address, has internet access, and has navigated to the StockLens web application. |  |
| **Main flow:** User launches the StockLens web application. User clicks the “Sign Up” button on the landing page. System displays the user registration form. User fills in the required details (full name, email address, password, confirm password). User reads and agrees to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy by ticking the consent checkbox. User acknowledges the educational-purpose disclaimer required under the Monetary Authority of Singapore Financial Advisers Act. User clicks the “Register” button. System validates the input (email format, password strength, email uniqueness, matching password confirmation). System creates a Base User account and persists the record in the Supabase PostgreSQL database. System sends a verification email to the registered address. System automatically signs the user in and redirects to the user dashboard. End. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None. |  |
| **Alternative / Exceptional flow:** 4a. Missing mandatory fields or invalid input format: i. System displays an inline error message next to each affected field. ii. Return to Step 4\. 5a. User does not agree to the Terms of Service: i. System disables the “Register” button until the consent checkbox is ticked. ii. Return to Step 5\. 8a. Email address already registered: i. System displays the message: “An account with this email already exists. Please log in or reset your password.” ii. Return to Step 4\. 8b. Password does not meet strength requirements: i. System displays the message: “Password must be at least 8 characters long and include at least one letter and one number.” ii. Return to Step 4\. 9a. Database write failure: i. System displays a generic error message and prompts the user to retry. ii. System logs the failure for administrator review. iii. Return to Step 7\.  |  |

**4.3.2 Base User Use Case Description**

| Name: Base User Login | ID: \#2 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To log in to system |  |
| **Description:** Base User inputs their log in credentials and clicks the “Login” button to access the system. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User enters their email and password and clicks the “Login” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Base User’s account has been successfully created. |  |
|  **Main Flow:** Base User opens the webpage. System display fields for username and password. Base User inputs username and password. Base user clicks the “Log In” button. System validates their credentials Base User is directed to homepage Base User is prompted to upgrade their membership System displays membership upgrade options. Base User selects whether to upgrade now or continue with limited access. If Base User chooses to upgrade, System directs them to the membership upgrade/payment page. If Base User chooses not to upgrade, System directs them to the homepage with limited features.  |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Premium User enters invalid, blank or wrong username or password. 6a. System display error message “Incorrect username or password, please try again.” 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4\. |  |

| Name: Base User Logout | ID: \#3 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To securely logout of the system |  |
| **Description:** Base User clicks the “Logout” button to end the session. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When the Base User clicks the “Logout” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Base User is currrently logged into the system. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium user clicks the “Logout” button. System ends the session and redirects the basic user to log in page |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None  |  |

| Name: Base User Search Stock | ID: \#4 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To search for a specific stock in the system |  |
| **Description:** Base User enters a stock name or ticker symbol into the search bar to retrieve stock information. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User enters a search query and initiates the search. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged into the system. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to the search bar. System displays the search input field. Base User enters a stock name or ticker symbol. Base User clicks the "Search" button or presses Enter. System retrieves and displays matching stock results. |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. No matching stocks are found for the entered query. 6a. System displays message: “No results found. Please try another search term” 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4  |  |

| Name: Base User Favourite Stock | ID: \#5 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To add a stock to their favourites list |  |
| **Description:** Base User marks a stock as a favourite by clicking the favourite icon on a stock listing or detail page. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User clicks the "Favourite" or star icon on a stock. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in and has accessed a stock listing page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User searches for or navigates to a stock. System displays the stock information with a "Favourite" icon. Base User clicks the "Favourite" icon on the selected stock. System saves the stock to the Base User's favourites list. System updates the icon to reflect that the stock is now a favourite.  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 4a. Stock is already saved in the Base User’s favourite list. 5a. System removes the stock from favourites (toggle on and off behaviour) 6a. System updates the icon to indicate the stock is no longer a favourite.  |  |
| **Name:** Base User Search Favourite | **ID:** \#6 |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To search for a specific stock within their favourites list |  |
| **Description:** Base User uses a search function within the favourites section to locate a specific saved stock |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User enters a query into the search bar within the favourites section. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in and has at least one stock saved in their favourite list. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to the favourites section System displays the favourites list and a search bar. Base User enters a stock name or ticker symbol into their search bar. Base User clicks the “Search” button  System filters and displays matching stocks from the favourites list. |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. No matching stocks are found within their favourites list. 6a. System displays message: “No matching favourites found. Please enter another search term.” 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4\. |  |

| Name: Base User View Date’s Top Gainers/Losers | ID: \#7 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To view the top gaining and losing stocks for a selected date. |  |
| **Description:** Base User selects a specific date to view which stock gained or lost the most value on that trading day. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User selects a date and clicks “View” in the Top Gainers/Losers section. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in and market data is available for the selected date. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to the "Top Gainers/Losers” section. System displays the section with a date selector, defaulted to the current trading day’s top gainers and losers Base User selects a specific date from the calendar Base User clicks the “View” button. System retrieves and displays the top gaining and losing stocks for that selected date |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. No market data is available for the selected date (i.e weekend or public holiday) 6a. System displays message: “No market data is available for the selected date. Please choose a valid trading day.” 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4\. |  |

| Name: Base User Update Password | ID: \#8 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To update their account password |  |
| **Description:** Base User changes their existing password and provides a new one. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User submits the password update form. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in and is on their “Account Settings” page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to “Account Settings” page. System displays the password update form which include the fields for current password, new password and confirm new password. Base User enters their current password. Base User enters their new password. Base User re-enters their new password in the confirm new password field. Base User clicks the “Update Password” button. System validates the current password and confirms that the new passwords match. System saves the new password and displays: “Password successfully updated.” |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:** 7a. The current password entered is incorrect. 8a. System displays: “Password entered was incorrect. Please try again.”9a. Repeat steps 3 to 6\. 7b. New password and confirmation of new password do not match. 8b. System displays: “Passwords do not match. Please retry again.” 9b. Repeat steps 3 to 6\. 7c. New password does not meet the security requirements (e.g minimum length, alphanumeric with 1 special case) 8c. System displays: “Password does not meet the security requirements. Please enter a password with at least one uppercase letter, number and special character.” 9c. Repeat steps 4 to 6\.  |  |

| Name: Base User View User Profile | ID: \#9 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To view their account profile information |  |
| **Description:** Base User navigates to the Profile Page to view their stored personal information and account details |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User navigates to the profile page. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User clicks on the “Profile” menu item or icon. System retrieves the Base User’s profile data. System displays the Base User’s profile information including name, email and account type. |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None |  |

| Name: Base User Update Profile | ID: \#10 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To update their personal profile information. |  |
| **Description:** Base User edits and saves changes to their profile details |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User submits the updated profile form. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in and is on their profile page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to the “Profile” page. System displays an editable form pre-populated with the Base User’s current profile information. Base User updates the desired fields. Base User clicks the “Save Changes” button. System validates the updated information. System saves the changes and displays: “Profile updated successfully.” |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Base User submits the form with invalid or blank required fields. 6a. System displays an error message indicating which fields need to be corrected. 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4\.  |  |

| Name: Base User View Subscription Status | ID: \#11 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Base User – To update their current subscription plan and status. |  |
| **Description:** Base User navigates to the Subscription section to view details about their subscription plan. |  |
| **Actors:** Base User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Base User navigates to the Subscription page |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Base User is logged in. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Base User navigates to the “Subscription and Billing” in account settings. System retrieves the Base Use’s subscription data. System displays the message: “You currently have no active subscription.” System displays the different subscription plans which include the plan type and included features. |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None |  |

**4.3.3 Premium User Use Case Description**

| Name: Premium User Log In | ID: \#12 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To log in to system |  |
| **Description:** Premium User inputs their log in information and clicks “Log In” button to access system. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User enters log in information and clicks “Log In” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User’s account has been successfully created. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User open the webpage. System display fields for username and password. Premium User inputs username and password. Premium user clicks “Log In” button. System validates username and password. Premium User is directed to homepage |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Premium User enters invalid, blank or wrong username or password. 6a. System display error message “Incorrect username or password, please try again.” 7a. Repeat steps 3 and 4\. |  |
| **Name:** Premium User Log Out | **ID: \#13** |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To log out to system |  |
| **Description:** Premium User clicks “Log Out” button to end the session. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks “Log Out” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User has logged into system. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium user clicks “Log Out” button. System ends session and redirects premium user to log in page |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 1a. Session timeout, system automatically logs out premium user from system and redirects to log in page.  |  |

| Name: Premium User Search Stock | ID: \#14 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To search stocks in system |  |
| **Description:** Premium User inputs the search details and click “Search” button on stocks page |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User enters search details and clicks “Search” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the stock page |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on stock page. System display input fields for search details. Premium User inputs search details. Premium user clicks “Search” button. System displays stocks with corresponding search details. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Premium User enters invalid, blank or wrong search details. 6a. System display “No Stocks Found” |  |

| Name: Premium User Favorite Stock | ID: \#15 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To favorite stock to save stock into watchlist |  |
| **Description:** Premium User favorite stock to save stock into a watchlist for easy access. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks “Favorite” button on the stock detail’s page. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the stock detail’s page |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User opens stock details page. System display stock details and a “Favorite” button. Premium user clicks “Favorite” button. System marks stock as favorited for Premium User and saved into watchlist System display “Stock Favorited” button. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Premium User clicks “Stock Favorited” button 6a. System marks stock as not favorited, and removes from watchlist 7a. System display “Favorite” button, return to step 2\. |  |

| Name: Premium User Search Favorite Stock | ID: \#16 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To search favorite stock in watchlist |  |
| **Description:** Premium User search favorite stock in watchlist for easy access. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User enters search details and clicks “Search” button. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the watchlist page |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on watchlist page. System display input fields for search details. Premium User input search details. Premium User clicks “Search” button. System displays stocks with corresponding search details. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** 5a. Premium User enters invalid, blank or wrong search details. 6a. System display “No Stocks Found” |  |

| Name: Premium User View Favorite Stock | ID: \#17 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To view favorite stock in watchlist |  |
| **Description:** Premium User view favorite stock details in watchlist. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on a favorite stock on watchlist page. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the watchlist page |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on watchlist page. System display watchlist with all favorite stock of premium user. Premium User clicks on a favorite stock. System redirects user to stock details page and displays stock details of the favorite stock. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None  |  |

| Name: Premium User View date’s gainers / losers | ID: \#18 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To view date’s gainers / losers |  |
| **Description:** Premium User view date’s gainers / losers. (the top and lowest performing stocks of the day.) |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on “Today’s Top Gainers / Losers” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the homepage. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on homepage. System displays a widget with today’s top gainers / loser. Premium User clicks on the widget. System redirects user to top gainers / losers page. System displays today’s top gainers / losers. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None  |  |

| Name: Premium User Update Password | ID: \#19 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To update password |  |
| **Description:** Premium User to change current password to a new password. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on “update password” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the user profile page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on user profile page. System displays inputs fields for Update Password. Premium User enters new password. Premium User clicks “Update Password” button. System displays Password successfully updated. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:**  5a. Systems display error if password format is incorrect. 6a. Repeat step 3 and 4\. |  |

| Name: Premium User View User Profile | ID: \#20 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To View User Profile |  |
| **Description:** Premium User to view user profile. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on profile icon. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User logged into system. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is logged into system. System displays user profile icon on top right of page. Premium User clicks profile icon. System redirects premium user to user profile page. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:** None |  |

| Name: Premium User Update User Profile | ID: \#21 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To update User Profile |  |
| **Description:** Premium User to update user profile details. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on “update details” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the user profile page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on user profile page. System displays inputs fields for user details. Premium User enters new user details. Premium User clicks “Update Details” button. System displays details successfully updated. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:**  5a. Systems display error if details format is incorrect. 6a. Repeat step 3 and 4\. |  |

| Name: Premium User Unsubscribe from premium | ID: \#22 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To Unsubscribe from Premium |  |
| **Description:** Premium User to Unsubscribe from Premium |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on “Unsubscribe” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the user profile page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on user profile page. System displays “Unsubscribe” button. Premium User clicks “Unsubscribe” button. System displays successfully unsubscribe. Premium user is now a basic user. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:**  None |  |

| Name: Premium User Customizable prediction | ID: \#23 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Premium User – To customize predictions |  |
| **Description:** Premium User to change inputs for final model prediction on stocks. |  |
| **Actors:** Premium User |  |
| **Trigger:** When Premium User clicks on “customize prediction” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Premium User is on the user profile page. |  |
| **Main Flow:** Premium User is on stock details page. System displays inputs fields for customizable prediciton. Premium User adjust the input data. Premium User clicks “Customizable Prediction” button. System displays new prediction based on adjusted data. |  |
| **Sub-flow:** None |  |
| **Alternate Flow:**  5a. Systems display error if input data format is incorrect. |  |

**4.3.4 User Admin Use Case Description**

| Name: User Admin Login | ID: \#24 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, Securely authenticate and access administrative features |  |
| **Description:** Allow the user admin to authenticate themself and securely access the maintenance side of the website |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** The user admin navigates to the admin login portal |  |
| **Pre-condition:** An existing and active account must be created for the user admin |  |
|  **Main Flow:**  1\. The user admin enters their account credentials2\. System verifies the credentials against the database3\. The system logs the user admin in and directs them to the admin dashboard  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:** **Invalid Credentials:** If the credentials do not match, the system displays an "Invalid username or password" error message and prompts the user admin to try again.  |  |

| Name: User Admin Logout | ID: \#25 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, securely end their session to protect the security of the system while they are away |  |
| **Description:** Allow the user admin to safely terminate the active session |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** When the user admin clicks on the “Logout” button |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must already be logged in to the system |  |
|  **Main Flow:** 1\. The User Admin clicks on the logout button 2\. The system stops the active session token 3\. The system redirects the User Admin to the login screen  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:** None  |  |

| Name: User Admin View Account | ID: \#26 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, Locate and review specific user account details or monitor the user base |  |
| **Description:** Enables the user admin to browse, search and view the details of user accounts within the system |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** User admin clicks on the “user management” navigation link |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must be logged in with appropriate viewing privileges |  |
|  **Main Flow:** 1\. The user admin navigates to the accounts page 2\. The system retrieves and displays a list of all user accounts 3\. The user admin clicks on a specific account to view its full profile 4\. The system displays the detailed account information  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:**  No results: If the User Admin uses a search bar and no accounts match the criteria, the system  displays a "No accounts found" message.  |  |

| Name: User Admin Update Account | ID: \#27 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, to keep user data accurate |  |
| **Description:** Allow the user admin to modify user profile details for existing user accounts |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** User admin clicks on the edit or update page |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must be logged in with appropriate update privileges, target user account must exist |  |
|  **Main Flow:** 1\. The User Admin selects an account to edit 2\. The system displays a form pre-populated with the user's current data 3\. The User Admin modifies the necessary fields and clicks "Save" 4\. The system validates the new data and updates the database 5\. The system displays a success message confirming the update  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** Changes made will be logged in an administrative audit log  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:** Validation failure: If the user admin enters invalid data, the system will highlight the error and prompt the user admin for a correct input  |  |

| Name: User Admin Suspend Account | ID: \#28 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, Disable a user’s access to the system |  |
| **Description:** Allow the user admin to temporarily disable a user’s access to the system without deleting any user data |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** User admin selects the suspend action on an active user’s profile |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must be logged in, target user is currently active |  |
|  **Main Flow:** 1\. The User Admin selects an active account to suspend 2\. The system prompts for confirmation and reason for suspension 3\. The User Admin confirms the action 4\. The system changes the account status to "Suspended" and revokes any active session tokens for that user 5\. The system displays a success message.  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:**  Action cancelled: If the user admin cancels while at the confirmation prompt, the system aborts the process and returns to the account view  |  |

| Name: User Admin Unsuspend Account | ID: \#29 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, Restore access for users who have previously been suspended |  |
| **Description:** Allows the user admin to restore system access to a previously suspended account |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** User admin selects the “Unsuspend” action on a suspended user profile |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must be logged in, the target user must currently be suspended |  |
|  **Main Flow:** 1\. The User Admin selects a suspended account 2\. The system prompts for confirmation to restore access 3\. The User Admin confirms the action 4\. The system changes the account status back to "Active" 5\. The system displays a success message.  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:** Action Cancelled: If the User Admin cancels at the confirmation prompt, the system aborts the process and leaves the account suspended.  |  |

| Name: User Admin Reset Password | ID: \#30 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** User admin, Assist users with the changing of their passwords |  |
| **Description:** Allows the user admin to manually trigger a password reset process for specific users |  |
| **Actors:** User admin |  |
| **Trigger:** User admin selects the “Reset password” button on a user’s account |  |
| **Pre-condition:** User admin must be logged in, target user account must exist |  |
|  **Main Flow:**  1\. The User Admin requests a password reset for a specific account 2\. The system asks for confirmation 3\. The User Admin confirms 4\. The system generates a secure, one-time password reset link 5\. The system emails the link directly to the user's registered email address 6\. The system notifies the User Admin that the reset email was successfully sent  |  |
|  **Sub-flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternate Flow:**  Missing Email Address: If the user account does not have a valid email on file, the system alerts the User Admin and generates a temporary password on the screen for the Admin to manually communicate to the user.  |  |

     **4.3.5 Backend Admin Use Case Description** 

| Name: Admin – Log In | ID: \#31 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Backend Admin – To log in to the administrative portal. |  |
| **Description:** Admin enters administrative credentials to access the backend management dashboard. |  |
| **Actors:** Backend Admin |  |
| **Trigger:** When the Admin accesses the admin URL and enters credentials. |  |
| **Pre-condition:** The Admin account exists with administrative privileges. |  |
| **Main flow:** Admin launches the admin portal.   System logs out the admin.   Admin is redirected to the login screen.   Admin clicks the "Log In" button.   System validates credentials and admin role.   Admin is redirected to the Backend Dashboard.   End.   |  |
|  **Sub – flow:** None |  |
|  **Alternative Flow:**  5.1a. Invalid credentials: System displays "Access Denied." 5.1b. Repeat steps 3 and 4\.  |  |

     
 

| Name: Adjust Default Customizable Prediction Weightage | ID: \#32 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Backend Admin – To fine-tune the global prediction algorithm settings. |  |
| **Description:** Admin modifies the weightage of different variables used in the system's stock prediction model. |  |
| **Actors:** Backend Admin |  |
| **Trigger:** Admin navigates to "Algorithm Settings." |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Admin is logged in and the system is operational. |  |
|  **Main flow:**   1\. Admin selects "Adjust Prediction Weightage" from the dashboard. 2\. System displays current weightage parameters (e.g., Technicals, Sentiments). 3\. Admin modifies the percentage values. 4\. Admin clicks "Save Changes." 5\. System validates that total weightage equals 100%. 6\. System updates the global prediction model. 7\. End.    |  |
|  **Sub – flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternative Flow:**  5.1a. Weightage total \= 100%: System prompts "Total must equal 100%." 5.1a. Admin adjusts values and clicks "Save." |  |

 

| Name: Add Market Sentiment Analysis Watchlist | ID: \#33 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Backend Admin – To add new keywords or sources for sentiment tracking. |  |
| **Description:** Admin adds specific tickers or keywords to the system's sentiment analysis engine.  |  |
| **Actors:** Backend Admin |  |
| **Trigger:** Admin selects "Add Watchlist Item." |  |
| **Pre-condition:** Admin is logged in. |  |
|  **Main flow:**   1\. Admin navigates to the Sentiment Management section. 2\. Admin clicks "Add New Watchlist." 3\. Admin enters the ticker symbol and relevant social media/news keywords. 4\. Admin clicks "Submit." 5\. System verifies the ticker exists in the database. 6\. System starts crawling data for the new entry. 7\. End.   |  |
|  **Sub – flow:** None  |  |
| **Alternative Flow:**  5.1a. Duplicate Entry: System prompts "Keyword/Ticker already exists in watchlist."   |  |

| Name: Generate Periodic Report | ID: \#34 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Stakeholders and goals:** Backend Admin – To extract system performance and user activity data. |  |
| **Description:** Admin generates a PDF or CSV report based on a selected timeframe. |  |
| **Actors:** Backend Admin |  |
| **Trigger:** Admin clicks "Generate Report." |  |
| **Pre-condition:** None. |  |
|  **Main flow:**   1\. Admin navigates to the Reports section. 2\. Admin selects the report type (e.g., Stock Accuracy, User Growth). 3\. Admin selects the date range (Start Date \- End Date). 4\. Admin clicks "Download Report." 5\. System compiles the data and generates the file. 6\. End.   |  |
|  **Sub – flow:** None  |  |
|  **Alternative Flow:** None  |  |

# **5\. Development Methodology** {#5.-development-methodology}

\[This section should describe the development methodology used for the project, including the project management, deliverables as well as quality assurance aspects.\]

| Front-end  |  |
| ----- | :---: |
| Development Tools  | Visual Studio Code  |
| Language  | JavaScript/TypeScript |
| Framework  | React.js |
| Description |  |
| React.js is an open-source front-end JavaScript library developed and maintained by Meta. It enables developers to build interactive user interfaces (UIs) for web and mobile applications. React.js allows developers to create reusable UI elements, which makes the development process more structured and efficient. It is well-suited for single-page applications, where users can interact with the system without requiring to reload the webpage. |  |
| Back-End  |  |
| Development Tools  | Visual Studio Code |
| Language  | Python |
| Framework / Libraries | Tensorflow, Pytorch |
| Description  |  |
| Python is used for the back-end development of our web application due to its widespread use in machine learning, artificial intelligence, and data processing. The back-end is responsible for handling core systems logic, which includes processing user requests, managing dataflow, and running machine learning models. TensorFlow and PyTorch are machine learning libraries used to develop, train, test and deploy models, enabling features such as prediction, classification and data analysis.  Python’s extensive ecosystem of machine learning libraries and its compatibility with web frameworks make it a natural fit for this project.  |  |
| Database  |  |
| Development tools  | Supabase, Visual Studio Code  |
| Language   | SQL  |
| Database Platform | Supabase |
| Description  |  |
| Supabase is an open-source Backend as a Service (BaaS) platform built on PostgreSQL. It is designed as a modern, SQL-based alternative to firebase. It provides developers with a variety of services including database management, user authentication, and API services for web applications. This is all accessible through an intuitive dashboard.Why Supabase? Easy integration with React.js Provides a PostgreSQL relational database Supports real-time data updates Includes built-in user authentication Provides automatic API generation Scalable Open-Source and developer friendly |  |

**GanttPro**   
We are using GanttPro as our online project management tool to plan, schedule, and track our project progress through interactive Gantt charts. It helps our team organize tasks, set deadlines, manage dependencies and milestones, and collaborate more effectively to ensure the project stays on track and is completed on time.

![][image14]  
![][image15]

# **6\. Expected Results** {#6.-expected-results}

| Goal | Expected Result |
| ----- | ----- |
| **Stock Market Prediction** | Users receive predicted stock movement, such as positive/negative trend direction and estimated opening or closing price. |
| **Technical Analysis** | The system analyses historical stock price data, chart patterns, and technical indicators to generate a prediction or confidence score. |
| **Sentiment Analysis** | The system analyses tweets, posts, and public statements from influential or political figures to identify positive, neutral, or negative market sentiment. |
| **Company forecast analysis** | The system considers company-related data such as financial performance, news, or forecast indicators to support prediction accuracy. |
| **Combined prediction model** | Outputs from technical analysis, sentiment analysis, and company forecast analysis are combined to produce an overall prediction and confidence score. |
| **Real-time data access** | The webservice retrieves updated stock and market-related data through RESTful APIs for use in prediction and analysis. |
| **User-friendly dashboard** | Users can view individual analysis results, overall predictions, confidence scores, and supporting data in a clear web interface. |
| **Decision support** | The system helps users make more informed investment-related decisions by presenting data-driven insights, while not guaranteeing market outcomes. |

# **7\. Conclusion** {#7.-conclusion}

This project is expected to produce a webservice that supports stock market prediction by combining technical analysis, sentiment analysis, and company forecast analysis into a single decision-support platform. By using RESTful APIs to collect market and external data, the system can process different types of information and present users with individual model outputs, an overall prediction, and a confidence score.

Overall, the project aims to demonstrate how machine learning and web technologies can be applied to financial market analysis in a practical and accessible way. While the system cannot guarantee accurate stock market predictions due to market uncertainty and external factors, it can help users better understand market trends, compare different prediction signals, and make more informed decisions based on data-driven insights.
